verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46) is a type-I single-pass TM glycoprotein whose canonical steady-state localization is the trans-Golgi network, where its TM domain and cytoplasmic tail encode retention/retrieval signals. However, the protein is well-documented to cycle between the TGN and the plasma membrane via endosomes. The surface pool is a minority fraction arising from active vesicular recycling: the protein transiently reaches the outer-leaflet-accessible PM before being retrieved back to the TGN via clathrin/adaptor-mediated endocytosis. Because it has its own TM anchor and a documented PM transit, but the dominant compartment is endomembrane, this maps to contextual/dual_localization.
